title: Replace Upper and Lower Plastic Blocks
summary: Remove a worn or damaged upper or lower plastic block on the tipper and install the replacement block using the documented M6 socket-head screws, then restart the operator station. The source identifies required PPE and tools, requires LOTO, and references Figure 7-11 for block location and mounting points.
when_to_use: Use when an upper or lower plastic block on the tipper must be replaced due to wear or damage, using the documented replacement procedure in section 7.3.11.1.
not_for: Do not use for general operator execution; the procedure includes LOTO and component replacement.
Do not use when the performer does not have access to the referenced LOTO procedure.
Do not use when the performer does not have access to the referenced operator station startup procedure.
Do not use if the block location, orientation, or mounting arrangement cannot be confirmed from the source-backed figure.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform LOTO, remove the existing plastic block, install the replacement block with the documented M6 screws, and restart the operator station per referenced procedure.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper before performing the replacement.
This is not a support-safe procedure for general operator execution.
The source references separate procedures for lockout/tagout and operator station startup but does not restate those steps here.
access_or_tools_needed: Replacement upper plastic block or lower plastic block
Access to the referenced LOTO procedure
Access to the referenced operator station startup procedure

access_required: Access to the referenced LOTO procedure
Access to the referenced operator station startup procedure
system_preconditions: The correct replacement upper or lower plastic block is available.
The plastic block to be replaced has been identified.
external_references: Referenced LOTO procedure for the tipper

visual_references.description: Figure 7-11 showing the upper and lower plastic blocks and their M6x16 socket-head screw mounting points.

steps.title: Gather required replacement parts, PPE, and tool
steps.purpose: Ensure all documented items are available before beginning the replacement.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement upper or lower plastic block, safety glasses, gloves, and a 5-mm hex wrench.
steps.expected_result: All required replacement items, PPE, and the documented tool are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct replacement block, safety glasses, gloves, and 5-mm hex wrench are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: Any required replacement item, PPE, or the 5-mm hex wrench is missing or the correct replacement block cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the documented required items: replacement upper or lower plastic block, safety glasses, gloves, and a 5-mm hex wrench.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use Figure 7-11 to confirm the component being replaced is the upper or lower plastic block covered by this procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct replacement upper or lower plastic block is not available.
Required PPE or the 5-mm hex wrench is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The nearby procedure states that required items include replacement upper plastic blocks and lower plastic blocks, safety glasses, gloves, and a 5-mm hex wrench, with an estimated time of 15 minutes.

steps.title: LOTO the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in the required safe state before removing or installing the plastic block.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper as referenced by the manual before continuing with the replacement.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and tagged out per the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been completed and the tipper is in the required maintenance-safe state.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO cannot be completed, cannot be verified, or the referenced LOTO procedure is not available.

actions.label: Perform LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper as referenced by the manual.

stop_or_escalate_if: The documented LOTO reference is not available to the person performing the work.
LOTO cannot be completed or verified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The replacement steps instruct the user to LOTO the tipper.

steps.title: Locate the plastic block and identify its mounting screws
steps.purpose: Confirm the correct block and the documented fasteners before removal.
steps.instruction: Locate the upper or lower plastic block to be replaced and identify the M6 socket-head screws securing it in place. Use Figure 7-11 to confirm block location and mounting points.
steps.expected_result: The correct plastic block and its securing M6 socket-head screws have been identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The block location and screw mounting points match the source-backed figure.
steps.failure_condition: The block location, orientation, or mounting arrangement cannot be confirmed from the figure and procedure.

actions.label: Identify block and screws
actions.exact_action: Locate the plastic block to be replaced and identify the M6 socket-head screws securing it in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Upper plastic block location, lower plastic block location, and the M6x16 socket-head screws securing the block.

stop_or_escalate_if: The block location cannot be confirmed from Figure 7-11.
The block orientation or mounting arrangement cannot be confirmed from the source-backed figure and procedure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-11 Plastic Blocks appears on page 154... showing the upper and lower plastic blocks and their M6x16 socket-head screw mounting points.
Figure 7-11 appears on page 154 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_11_1_replacement.

steps.title: Remove the existing plastic block fasteners
steps.purpose: Free the existing plastic block so it can be replaced.
steps.instruction: Remove all of the M6 socket-head screws securing the plastic block in place.
steps.expected_result: All documented M6 socket-head screws securing the plastic block have been removed.
steps.healthy_condition: The plastic block is no longer secured by the M6 screws and is ready for replacement.
steps.failure_condition: One or more securing screws cannot be removed or the fastener arrangement does not match the documented configuration.

actions.label: Remove M6 screws
actions.exact_action: Remove all of the M6 socket-head screws securing the plastic block in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The M6x16 socket-head screw locations securing the plastic block.

stop_or_escalate_if: The securing screws cannot be identified with confidence.
One or more M6 socket-head screws cannot be removed.
The fastener arrangement does not match the source-backed figure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The replacement steps instruct the user to remove all M6 socket-head screws securing the plastic block in place.

steps.title: Install the new plastic block
steps.purpose: Mount the replacement block using the documented hardware.
steps.instruction: Mount the new block using the removed M6 screws. Use Figure 7-11 to confirm mounting points and component orientation.
steps.expected_result: The new plastic block is mounted in place using the removed M6 screws.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement block is installed in the documented location and orientation, with the removed M6 screws used for mounting.
steps.failure_condition: The new block cannot be mounted correctly, the orientation cannot be confirmed, or the mounting arrangement does not match the source-backed figure.

actions.label: Mount replacement block
actions.exact_action: Mount the new block using the removed M6 screws.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Mounting points and component orientation for the replacement upper or lower plastic block.

stop_or_escalate_if: The block orientation cannot be confirmed from Figure 7-11.
The mounting arrangement cannot be confirmed from the source-backed figure and procedure.
The new block cannot be mounted using the removed M6 screws.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The replacement steps instruct the user to mount the new block with the removed M6 screws.
Figure 7-11 appears on page 154 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_11_1_replacement.

steps.title: Restart the operator station
steps.purpose: Return the station to service using the referenced startup procedure after replacement is complete.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station as referenced by the manual.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is restarted after the replacement is completed.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station restart has been completed using the referenced procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The documented restart reference is not available or the operator station cannot be restarted as referenced.

actions.label: Restart operator station
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station as referenced by the manual.

stop_or_escalate_if: The documented operator station startup reference is not available to the person performing the work.
The operator station cannot be restarted as referenced by the manual.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The replacement steps instruct the user to restart the operator station.

success_criteria: The replacement plastic block is mounted in place with the documented M6 screws.
The installed block location and mounting arrangement match Figure 7-11.
healthy_conditions: Required PPE, replacement block, and 5-mm hex wrench are available before work begins.
The tipper is LOTO'd before component removal.
The correct upper or lower plastic block and its M6 screw mounting points are confirmed using Figure 7-11.
The new block is mounted using the removed M6 screws.
The operator station is restarted using the referenced procedure.
failure_conditions: The correct replacement block, PPE, or required tool is unavailable.
LOTO cannot be completed or verified.
The block location, orientation, or mounting arrangement cannot be confirmed from the source-backed figure and procedure.
The M6 socket-head screws cannot be removed or reused as documented.
The referenced operator station startup procedure is unavailable or restart cannot be completed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the block location, orientation, or mounting arrangement cannot be confirmed from the source-backed figure and procedure.
Escalate if the documented restart or lockout/tagout references are not available to the person performing the work.
Escalate if the securing M6 screw arrangement does not match Figure 7-11 or the documented procedure.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Upper and lower plastic block locations and M6x16 socket-head screw mounting points.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-11 appears on page 154 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_11_1_replacement.

missing_details: The packet does not include the full text of section 7.3.11.1; step wording is grounded from the candidate and artifact retrieval text.
The source references separate LOTO and operator station startup procedures, but their detailed steps are not included in this packet.
The packet does not provide explicit torque values or verification checks for the M6 screws.
The packet does not state whether production stop is required beyond the LOTO requirement.
